reasoning:
The baseline 15-year mortgage PMMS rate (applicable for 2025-05-25) is 6.01%, established by the week ending May 22 release. 
1. 2025-06-01: This resolves with the May 29 release. Daily tracker rates from May 20-23 showed 15-year rates rising, hitting up to 6.09%, reflecting mid-May's jump in the 10-year Treasury yield to ~4.54%. There is a reasonably strong case (p=0.63) that the May 29 average clears the 6.01% threshold. 
2. 2025-06-24: Slightly lower upward momentum as the immediate effect of the yield spike settles, but still more likely than not to stay elevated (p=0.58).
3. 2025-08-23: Broad market forecasts anticipate 15-year mortgage rates to fall to around 5.75% for 2025, suggesting a shift below 6.01%. Thus, the expectation turns negative (p=0.45).
4. 2025-11-21: The estimated mean continues to lean below 6.01%, but the increasing time horizon means a wider probability variance (greater uncertainty). Following instructions to pull distant probabilities closer to 0.5, we place our estimate at 0.48.
